17476899:
Queen Bes

3 locations:

  • Besmara's Temple
  • Queen's Processional
22474699:
Unaimo

Unaimo is a trade town on the Crown of the World, in the eastern Hoarwell March. It is constructed from mounds built into the bluffs on the shore of Lake Yamunaq near the Alasek Ridge, and serves the Path of Aganhei as the trade route climbs the nearby Koumssa Gap onto the High Ice.
28997549:
Stinger's Scar

Stinger's Scar was a park called Celestial Pleasure Ground where the elite could spend their time enjoying its verdant gardens, but when an earthquake struck the city, a vermin infestation appeared out of nowhere. From wasps to flies to worms, all kinds of insects began consuming the vegetation, and for some inexplicable reason, new species of these vermin kept appearing. The place is avoided by the neighborhood, which doesn't know why the insects are staying in the park or why they were attracted there, and researchers have observed humanoid shapes in the swarms of flies dancing without any human features on their faces. The central creature of these stories has been presented as the Scorpion Prince, and its nature is not just a story to tell children at night.
30118529:
Aysepir's Astounded Abyss

1 locations:
  • Little Inner Sea
40706629:
Starrise Spire

1 locations:
  • Academy of the Reclamation
46375109:
Manaket

The port city of Manaket on Rahadoum's northern Inner Sea coast was once famous for its lush gardens, but these all dried up a hundred years ago as the desert encroached ever further into the settlement.
54971589:
Tamrivena

The county town of Tamrivena is located in the western Ustalavic county of Canterwall, one of the Palatinates, in close proximity to the unsettling Shudderwood. The county of Tamrivena was one of the first to declare its independence from traditional rule after nearby Lozeri did so in 4670 AR, replacing its hereditary ruler with a Palatine Council in a bloodless coup and changing its name to Canterwall.
55251449:
Novoboro

The town of Novoboro, hidden behind a large fortified stone wall, is a trading post originally built by the elves in what is now Cordelon. It boasts a dozen timber buildings and a two-story stone tower, with archer lookouts on top watching the river on which the town is situated. The path to the town is marked by painted trees and tattered banners bearing the image of a crow. Crows are an important omen to the locals, and killing one is punishable by death.
56951409:
Seer's Home

Seer's Home, located on the western coast of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, is Icemark's capital but no more than a large village by population. However, it is the largest and only permanently inhabited Varki shelter, and home to the Varki Seers, wizened elders who have retired to this more permanent residence.
62149589:
Isle of the Dreamers

The Isle of the Dreamers is located in the eastern part of Lake Tapipallati, off the west-central coast of Xopatl. On this island the veil between the Universe and the Dreamlands is particularly thin, allowing easy access to people and for planar energy to seep through and transform local wildlife into alebrijes.
64520299:
Broken Bastion

The Broken Bastion was a clock tower maintained by a mystical sect obsessed with time, but three centuries ago the structure and the temporal artifact it protected were destroyed by the dragon Maejeryx Steeleye. Since then, strange temporal anomalies have occurred in the area, attracting the curiosity of Fourth Spell Lord Muar Gauthfallow, who resides in the complex. The sect constructed many chambers sealed with anti-divination magic to store dangerous magical artifacts, and Muar is putting them to good use by storing the dangerous contraband he acquires in the course of his duties, intended for use against any external magical threat. There is only one chamber that was already sealed before his arrival, and whenever a temporal anomaly occurs, the lock keeping it closed moves. Muar considers it his responsibility to be in the area in case whatever emerges from the chamber proves dangerous.
73347909:
Tyvas-Devas

Tyvas-Devas is an inland fortress town on the Devil's Arches island in the Shackles. It is made of stone by slave labor on the banks of the Holshod River. Arronax Endymion, the ruler of Devil's Arches, forbids outsiders from entering the town without his permission. The castle at the town's center is built as the admiral's redoubt should his Chelaxian enemies arrive.

Tyvas-Devas is overseen by Endymion's fiercely loyal lieutenant, Iome Paemadar. The black-clad summoner and her fearsome eidolon are known to walk the streets at night, making certain that everything is ready for her beloved admiral. The town is surrounded by rich farmland tended by slaves, which provides food crops for all of Devil's Arches. It is known that the admiral's great wealth lies in the cellars below the Tyvas-Devas' fortress.
74639819:
Mount Soryu

Mount Soryu is the 11th-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 14,603 feet. It is one of four mountains surrounding the city of Taggoret, alongside Mount Onik to its east, Mount Langley to its north, and Mount Carissa to its south.

The village of Scryer is located at its snowline and is home to stargazers who also watch the surrounding roads and passes.
75891159:
Leftover's

Leftover's is the only inn in the East Shore neighborhood of the Varisian city of Korvosa. It offers a dozen rooms big enough for two people to sleep in as well as a large, comfortable common room and charges standard rates. The innkeeper, Portenus Gaskelinni, is an old soldier who lost an arm in a fight with a Shoanti; this tale is often told by the innkeeper and re-enacted fortnightly by Gaskelinni's Korvosan Guard comrades in the inn as entertainment for the inn's guests.
85112229:
Bloody Tarn

The Bloody Tarn is the base of a tribe of redcaps who serve the Mother of Flies in the Hagwood. Like most of its members, they are violent towards all outsiders, attacking them unless they are invited by their mistress.
87482209:
Wreath of Stars

The Wreath of Stars is a circle of eight standing stones near the north-eastern borders of the Backar Forest believed to be dedicated to Desna due to the astronomical maps carved into the stone. In reality, the standing stones were used by fey when the connection to the First World was strongest in the area. A fey prince named Allarik built an underground dungeon connecting the entrance to the circle on the surface. When Earthfall occurred, most of the dungeon's residents perished, except for an ankou named Edani, who kills all who enter the dungeon. His reputation spread, and a cabal of cultists camped near the standing stones, considering him a harbinger of destruction.
100991509:
Mount Rinia

Mount Rinia is a 15,000-foot-tall peak in the western Menador Mountains, located in the Archduchy of Menador in Cheliax. It is the Maiestas River's source, and home to orcs, humans, and yrthaks.

The mountain is named for the subject of a Taldan ballad, a noblewoman who flung herself from a mountain to escape an arranged marriage, but sprouted wings and flew away to unknown freedom.
104785899:
Earthnavel

In the eastern Tusk Mountains of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords lies a deep pit that resembles an inverted 13-layer ziggurat. The layers are littered with the bones of massive animals; and the lower the layer, the larger the bones, until they dwarf those of the largest creatures known to walk the surface of Golarion today.

At the bottom of this pit is an opening just large enough to admit an average-sized humanoid, known as the Earthnavel, or sipapu to the local Kellid tribes. Below it stretch miles of freezing-cold tunnels which lead down into Nar-Voth, the upper layer of the Darklands. These passages show signs of collapse, suggesting that there may have once been larger tunnels here that extended beneath Nar-Voth into the deeper Darklands. It is possible many large beasts of the Tolguth in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords migrated there from Deep Tolguth in Orv via now-collapsed tunnels.
117733479:
Devil's Elbow Sea Cave

1 locations:
  • Zincher's Camp
124130449:
Blackblood Cay

Blackblood Cay is a small port town on Shark Island in the Shackles. It is located on a small hidden inlet of the island and was founded by Free Captain Simeon the Vain in 4703 AR. The port now hosts ten more pirate captains, while Simeon remains lord of Blackblood Cay. The town features berths along the wharf for only six vessels at a time.
124634129:
Magarai

Little is known about Magarai, a settlement of western Osirion during its First Age located in what is now Thuvia. The city was known for its zealous nature.
125566919:
Thunderscale Warrens

The Thunderscale Warrens are a large network of caves on Mediogalti Island, located less than a day's journey inland of the pirate city of Ilizmagorti. The warrens are home to the Thunderscale tribe, the largest of the island's tribes of kobolds. Numerous rumours about the caves make various claims; some suggest they are a source of gemstone or gold deposits, whilst others suspect they lead deep into the earth and perhaps link to the Darklands.
130320989:
Ridonport

Ridonport is a small coastal city located in the empire of Taldor, south of the city of Cassomir. The settlement is best known as the home of General Arnisant, the Taldan hero responsible for imprisoning Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Tyrant.
130999029:
College of Mysteries

3 locations:
  • Castle Blakros
  • Statue of Primarch Harvi
  • Uiry Manor Museum
142031239:
Spire of Nex

The Spire of Nex, also called the Tower of Nex, is a tall siege castle created by the archmage Nex as part of his unsuccessful siege of Absalom in 166 AR. Nex constructed it using a shard from his personal demiplane known as the Crux of Nex.
143617889:
Tirchin

Tirchin is the capital of the nation of Mishyria located on the southern Kardaji Bay coast of the country.
143868069:
Citadel Rivad

The oldest of the citadels of the Hellknights, Citadel Rivad squats in the Turanian Hills northwest of Westcrown, the Twilight City and former capital of Cheliax. It is a solemn jail to heretical scrolls, quartos, and banned lore, and is zealously guarded by the Order of the Rack.
153706099:
Grandfather's Arms

5 locations:
  • Eagle Garrison Fort
  • Famished Mind
  • Saddlebark Ranch
  • Wall of Faces
  • Weeping Grandfather
166497889:
Gerduhm

Gerduhm is an extremely secret dwarven fortress located high in the Tusk Mountains of the Hold of Belkzen, approximately 50 miles north of the Cenotaph. It is operated by the Oathkeepers, a society of dwarven priests of Kols who help maintain an ancient bloodline.
174604869:
District Courthouse

The District Courthouse in Eastgate has a reputation for the compassion shown by its judges and prosecutors, which makes sense given the close-knit nature of the district, but some of its sentences border on negligence. Samel Maleagant transfers the city's most depraved criminals to this courthouse, exploiting the situation to secure lighter sentences for his clients.
191734069:
Fusil

Fusil is a town in far-eastern Andoran surrounded on all sides by the Verduran Forest. Ages ago, the inhabitants of the hamlet made a pact with the local druids and fey-folk. In exchange for the location of a rich gem mine, the citizens of Fusil pledged that no one would chop down a single tree of the forest. The terms of the agreement have not been breached, and the inhabitants make a decent living from the mine, use only stone in the construction of their homes, and rely on peat fires for warmth.
202548759:
Ndele Gap

The Ndele Gap is a high-altitude mountain pass in western Nex between the Barrier Wall to the north and the Shattered Range to the south.

It is the most obvious and frequently travelled eastern route into the Mwangi Expanse, and Taldor's ill-fated Sixth Army of Exploration marched through the Gap on its way to disaster in the jungle.

The thin air and cold temperatures make travel difficult, and travellers are occasionally attacked by bandits and kholo.
228762219:
Petitioner's Port

Petitioner's Port is a town in southwestern Druma, in the region of Kahlehlin, near the border with Isger. It is well-connected to the rest of the country, serves as the southern gateway to Druma from Inner Sea nations, and has a reputation for initiating newcomers into the Prophecies of Kalistrade.
229376019:
Summershade Quarry

Summershade Granite Quarry is about seventy miles up the Katharevousa River from the Revousa Ditches. Once a significant source of granite for buildings in Whiterock and Kintargo, it was abandoned as uneconomical by House Jhaltero shortly before Ravounel's secession.
232936699:
Yiangma

Yiangma is a small tengu town in the nation of Kwanlai in Tian Xia. Situated in the Kimu Mountains, the isolated town is also near the border with Tianjing. The town is near a shrine dedicated to a group of fallen celestials.
249809769:
Gujaraldi

Gujaraldi, the capital of Xopatl, is located in the central part of the country.
273290389:
Sele

The red granite city of Sele in the Mwangi Expanse was once the site of an ancient Mauxi culture. The city's foundations were built on limestone, and eventually it sank beneath the waters of the Mbernbe River. This may have been a catalyst for the Mauxi migration into Thuvia. The only structure which remains above water—a domed palace—is home to a family of wyverns.
275996529:
Endiron School

The Endiron School in Eastgate is a school founded by a noble family with the aim of using words to resolve conflicts between people. To this end, the institution places great emphasis on languages, and its students must know two non-native languages ​​before graduating. Instructors from abroad come to share their culture, language, and food with the students, who often become merchants, diplomats, or travelers. The student body is composed of three-quarters Eastgate children who do not have to pay for their education, while the remaining third consists of children from abroad from families who have heard of the school's reputation and pay exorbitant tuition fees for their children to study there.
277719349:
Crowstump

Crowstump is a port town at the mouth of the Tourondel River where it enters Lake Encarthan. It serves as a waypoint for Mendevian Crusaders due to its proximity to southern Lastwall and lively selection of taverns, but is also known as a regional center of academia, trade market, and relatively peaceful settlement in a chaotic region.
297628769:
Sangoshi

Sangoshi was a metropolis on an island off the southeastern cost of Minkai before being overtaken by the Okaiyo Ocean hundreds of years ago. Its ruins are now a coral reef miles off the eastern shore of modern Minkai. Tales suggest the ruins rise from the depths for one day every 44 years.
317206329:
Forae Logos

320627059:
Lavieton

Lavieton is a small Andoran fishing village on the Inner Sea coast, approximately 250 miles southeast of the city of Augustana. The finest fish in all of the Inner Sea are said to be caught here, and the fishmonger at the Sign of the Blue Sail is known throughout the Inner Sea region. The best fishing is from midsummer through early autumn, after which Waldry Quence and the other workers at the Sign of the Blue Sail smoke the fish and prepare them for export. The houses of Lavieton are generally small and of wattle and daub construction, and sit on sandy dunes on the top of a seaside bluff.
336257189:
Altan Zuud

Altan Zuud is a passage through the Wall of Heaven mountains that connects the Crown of the World to the Tian Xia nation of Hongal. It is also called the Last Pass, or the Golden Pass, for being the final mountain pass on the Path of Aganhei.
349447949:
New Oppara

New Oppara (pronounced new oh-pa-ra) is the capital of the nation of Linvarre, formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar, on the continent of Tian Xia. It was rechristened in 7108 IC (4608 AR) by its colonial founder, General Orphyrea Amanandar, who defeated the local warlords who had previously controlled it. Its original Tien name was Kamikobu (pronounced ka-mi-koh-bu).

Positioned near the Ilushe River and bathed in its mists, New Oppara features architecture blended between Tian and Taldane styles. The city also includes the Raocado Market of hawkers and the Ocallio Institute of magical practice.
356980659:
House Beumhal

House Beumhal is located in south-western Odranto, one of the counties of Ustalav.
360010809:
Asuulek's Mouth

Asuulek's Mouth (sometimes spelt Asulek's Mouth) is the most active of Osirion's two volcanoes and the lair of the ancient red wyrm Asuulek, for which it is named. At its summit is a large magma lake, and the volcano frequently spews out toxic gases, ash, and lava bombs onto the nearby area. Small communities work the fertile soil around the base of Asuulek's Mouth, however, they do so with a watchful eye, keeping watch for both eruptions and dragons.

Some locations on Golarion are infused with elemental power and Asuulek's Mouth is one such location. Occasionally, a person living near Asuulek's Mouth will be touched by the fiery elemental energies of the volcano and gain the extraordinary ability to rapidly heal when covered in the ashes of destructive fire.
371138949:
Temple Canyon

Temple Canyon is a mysterious site on the southern Tandak Plains, north of the settlement of Old Sehir. This long, dry canyon is over 30 miles in length and at times reaches several hundred feet deep. In the earliest days of Taldor, Temple Canyon served as a burial site for the nation's emperors, aristocrats, and heroes of legend. The buried wealth of Temple Canyon was almost entirely picked clean thousands of years ago but the now empty tombs still serve as a repository of Taldor's ancient history.

Nowadays, the tombs of Temple Canyon are picked over by looters looking to carve away some of the ancient carvings and statuary in the vague hope of selling it on to Taldor's modern nobility. Lucky finds do still occur and in 4556 AR the tomb of Princess Modavora was discovered still intact, making the survivors of the expedition fantastically wealthy. Temple Canyon is considered a sacred site by House Stavian and the rangers who patrol its length happily execute any unauthorised intruders they catch within the canyon.
377032239:
Round Mountain

Round Mountain is an earthen sphere 10 miles in diameter that was dropped onto western Po Li in 7219 IC. It was formerly part of the Hao Jin Tapestry, where it was transported centuries ago from the Darklands of Tian Xia by Hao Jin to halt an invasion of the region by the ratfolk of Diguo-Dashu.
378876469:
Desert's Mouth

Desert's Mouth is a black pit at the bottom of a conical hole in the shifting sand dunes of the Osirian Desert near the Thuvian border. It resembles the lair of a giant ant lion and its circular hole is a strangely regular entrance into a huge cavern which is probably connected to the Darklands. Phosphorescent gases drift out of the pit at night and those who stay too near the pit report hallucinations about dark caves dripping with water. Descending the sandy slopes is difficult but ascending is almost impossible. The Desert's Mouth hasn't been explored in recent memory but ancient texts describe forests of glowing fungus and strange crustaceans skittering in the dark.

The Yerbira are said to use the Mouth to execute those who breach their few taboos. They lower the condemned into the pit with a rope and throw the rope after him. If the condemned can climb out the pit he is considered forgiven. No one has ever climbed out the pit so far.
380186869:
Vira Salisfer

Vira Salisfer is the social hub of Islatra, serving as the center of power for the small but surprisingly influential House Salisfer. The black walls are protected by barbicans, giving the place the impression of a keep.

419314609:
Reanimations

Reanimations is a store in the Ankar-Te Ring district of the Varisian city of Kaer Maga. It is owned and run by Gerik Mubb, and sells mindless undead to customers, who control them via death's head talismans. The shop also sells potions and other clerical services, which are generally of a higher standard than the undead.
422367869:
Table of Ralock

The Table of Ralock is a flat rock formation located on the plains south of Urgir, near the end of Flood Road. Under a policy first created by Grask Uldeth, at least one representative from each orc hold is to be stationed there with a retinue of bodyguards. These orcs offer their tribal tokens to those who wish to be under their protection in the nation, and the prices vary depending on the group and tribe.
437655079:
Otari

7 locations:

  • Dawnflower Library
  • Gauntlight Keep

441761809:
Crystalrock

Crystalrock is a large, natural cavern located in the Mindspin Mountains of south-eastern Varisia near the banks of the Falcon River. It was discovered by the dwarves of Janderhoff who also call it the "Heart of the World". Within the cavern is an enormous crystal formation which hangs suspended from crystal threads. Dwarven elders have gathered here annually for centuries to watch the formation shake and give off a low pulse which can be felt for miles around. Lately the "heartbeat" has been occurring more rapidly, a fact which has caused the dwarves much concern; at the moment, it is aligned to the changes of the seasons.

The cavern also gave its name to the Treaty of Crystalrock, signed here in 4472 AR between Janderhoff and the nearby Chelaxian city of Korvosa.
451346279:
Imperial Prison

The Imperial Prison is a massive structure near the western bank of the Nosam River. The facility houses most of Molthune's criminals, but in accordance with the nation's ideology, it is dedicated to the rehabilitation of prisoners so they can reintegrate into society and contribute to it. The guards are well-trained and assisted by teachers and clergy who offer their services in rehabilitation programs. The prison is divided into two areas: one for those who have committed minor crimes or who have not caused permanent harm, and the high-security zone, which contains the nation's most dangerous criminals.

The more progressive version of the prison works well, allowing inmates to receive education and skills training, and most become model citizens upon release. Warden Lonnox Rydel runs the prison, though he has neglected many of his duties, falsifying funds and selling prisoners to the hryngar for profit.
468139279:
Heggapnod

Heggapnod is a small village on the Cannibal Isles in the Shackles. Heggapnod has been at war with the neighboring village of Kukgukmol for years. The two villages rapidly lose men, and they will surely obliterate one another if nobody steps in to stop them. The Blood Queen enjoys this bloodshed and watches the weekly massacres through its kuru surrogates in each village.
472652669:
Frembrudd

The town of Frembrudd is located on the south-western cliffs of the island of Battlewall in the Linnorm Kingdom of the Ironbound Islands.
474550609:
Seventh Church of Iomedae

3 locations:
  • Blackfingers Temple
  • Shrine of the Failed
  • Tempering Hall
486178179:
Haigui Wan

Haigui Wan is a small town in Shenchu Bay island in the Shackles. It is built on the northeastern peninsula of the island to serve as the base of Shenchu's defensive fleet which protects the island from Dahak's Fang. Most of the fleet's ten ships are kobuksons ("turtle ships").
487381159:
Kalit

Kalit is a small village on the River Sphinx in Osirion.
487613949:
Kasai

Kasai is the capital of the Minkai empire on the continent of Tian Xia. The city was chosen as the capital at the founding of the empire in 6119 IC, succeeding Uddo, the former capital under the Teikoku Shogunate.
507428499:
Temple of the Whispering Spider

The Temple of the Whispering Spider is a ruined temple of Lao Shu Po, located in Eryiu Forest of northwestern Quain, that is now inhabited by an enormous arachnid said to be the spirit of a cursed ancient priestess or royal maiden who rejected being sacrificed to have her heart become a celestial pearl. Villages near the temple revere her as living testament of righteous defiance in the face of evil. They also say the arachnid is the only thing holding back the spirits believed to live within the temple and leave regular offerings of silver, forage, and meat at its entrance.
517409239:
Candlemere Tower

Candlemere Tower is a tower sacred to the cult of Yog-Sothoth, located in the Stolen Lands of the River Kingdoms. It has the power to open portals between planets if the correct rituals are performed on the night of the winter solstice. Recently, the investigator Olun Shandus, after learning of the tower's existence from a copy of the Pnakotic Manuscripts, travelled to Candlemere with a small group of companions, but their ritual proved faulty, depositing them on a world long consumed by bholes and allowing the bholes to burrow into the Stolen Lands.
523840199:
Saddlebark Ranch

Saddlebark Ranch is a breeder and trainer of faerie mounts and Taldan corgis located in the foothills of the Kortos Mounts on the Isle of Kortos. Its proprietor is Timblethorn Baublebrand, who launched the ranch after recognizing that riding dogs were more surefooted than axebeaks or camels in the Mounts.

Once an innovator, Saddlebark was as of 4720 AR merely one of a dozen such ranches in the region, and the Aspis Consortium had been bullying ranchers and acquiring property for a cut of the business.
527089909:
Berm

The Berm is a miles-long stand of semi-sentient trees that defend Kyonin's northern border with Razmiran along the Glass River. Nominally part of the northern Fierani Forest, and capable of independent movement, the Berm is greatly feared by the soldiers and agents of Razmir, who have never seen how it travels from place to place. Those who enter the forest are rarely seen again and their blood-soaked clothing is sometimes found downriver. The Berm's sudden morning appearance on what had been gentle plains the day before, is enough to give the stoutest Razmiri pause.
530484829:
Liferoot Stone

The Liferoot Stone is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower was a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers, though it is now partially collapsed.

The tower is located in the Swardlands on the Isle of Kortos, and is the southernmost of the towers, located near the town of Castinlee.
544051709:
Chasm of Screams

The Chasm of Screams is a ravine deep within the Mindspin Mountains, known for its labyrinthine caves and strong winds. Local tribes have attempted to settle in the area, but its dangers have led only the most daring to live there. The name comes from the series of screams that reverberate through the caves and are carried far by the wind, giving rise to rumors of monsters in the area that have made even more people avoid the area.
557071369:
Castle Firrine

Castle Firrine is an abandoned castle located just south of Ghostlight Marsh, at the edge of the Gravelands in what was once the nation of Lastwall.
560085989:
Delenah

Delenah is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located along the northernmost limits of the nation along its border with Taldor on the southern banks of the Jalrune River.
574299219:
Council Hall and Gaol Block

1 locations:
  • The Doomsayers' Stand
579952209:
Exemplary Execrables

5 locations:
  • Copper-Beater Hall
  • Eel's End
  • Jeggare's Jug
  • Sticky Mermaid
  • Temple of Aroden
582926969:
Band of the Palm Headquarters

The Band of the Palm Headquarters is where the Bloody Barbers criminal gang gathers for their operations throughout the city. Under the guise of the Band of the Palm, a union of chimney sweeps, garbage collectors, and cleaners operate out of this series of buildings that include offices, sleeping quarters, bankrupt businesses, and warehouses, all connected by ropes and bridges that make it difficult to distinguish one building from another. Legitimate members live in the area closest to the entrance, while below, the Barbers operate in a series of catacombs, sewers, and caverns guarded by the Ghostguides, a group of albino chokers who show the way to the base where the leaders reside—an impenetrable fortress. In a room hidden even from the guides, Dr. Bensi Skule experimented on a troll prisoner who would become the gang's leader.
586373859:
Vault of Abadar

The Vault of Abadar, also known as the Bank of Abadar, in Absalom's Ivy District is the most holy site to the faithful of Abadar.

While the Vault may appear to be unstable, it is sturdy and solid. Jostlin Ferqyr, councilwoman for the Ivy District, runs the vault like a merchant bank and provides venture capital to young merchants and artist groups.

Bor Dralfo, a paladin affiliated with Jostlin and the Vault, leads the Brotherhood of Abadar and enforces Ivy District rules to keep streets safe and clear, which has lead to tension with Nomarch Alain Always.
587145709:
Mines of Deeptreasure

The Deeptreasure Mining Company has a hidden and secret base known as the Mines of Deeptreasure that is located on the edge of the Bandu Hills. The company used indentured xorns, who worked under the company's promises to return them to the Plane of Earth, to obtain gemstones from the hills that were later sold in the Sargavan capital of Eleder, now known as Anthusis, Vidrian. The xorns were promised a way home once they supplied an unspecified number of gems, but hobgoblin explorers of the Stone Roads from Oprak who encountered and soon sought to undermine the company around 4721 AR then freed the xorn themselves.
606603359:
Cicatrix

The Cicatrix is a blasted wasteland on the southwestern edge of the Tanglebriar in the elven nation of Kyonin. Crowded with camouflaged oozes, it is avoided by both elves and the nearby demons.
608390349:
Langitheath

Langitheath was once a well-to-do manorial estate in the central Ustalavic province of Ardeal. It and the surrounding 900 acres of farmland were sold by the Loslimor family around ca. 4681 AR, after which the estate passed through a number of different owners before finally being abandoned. Despite the fact that no one is believed to have worked the land for decades, a new crop of flax still grows every year, forming strange spiral shapes around the old manor house. Those who have come close to the estate have reported seeing small figures capering through the fields, or report meeting unnatural children with eyes as cold and blue as the flax flowers of Langitheath.
608873899:
Evergreen Park

Evergreen Park, located in the Eastgate district, is the city's largest park, maintaining a perpetual spring thanks to the efforts of the Children of Spring, a Gozreh cult led by Lady Evigail of House Wycomb. At the center of the park is an open-air altar, often visited by sailors before embarking on voyages to distant lands.
611583199:
Kopparberget

Kopparberget is a huge open-pit copper mine located on the northern reaches of the Kodar Mountains in the Linnorm Kingdom of Southmoor. The mine sits at the point where several deep lucrative veins of metals converge. Although the mine has been in operation for many decades, it still provides a great portion of Linnorm Kingdoms' wealth.
622038649:
Thom

Thom is a small gnomish town in the central River Kingdoms.
629109699:
Sally Port

The Sally Port is the Sally Guard's base of operations and the gateway to the district, connecting it to Westerhold and Shoreline outside the city. The gate is specifically designed to facilitate cavalry charges, and tax collectors and bureaucrats record the trade passing through it, as the settlements of Otari, Galizhur, and Diobel use this gate to enter the city. A ditch surrounds the wall, and four drawbridges allow for adaptation to the city's defensive needs. Lady Seleenae regularly visits her office on the walls, where her financial acumen makes extensive use of the records of the economy flowing through the gate. Exports from the nearest settlements include fish from Shoreline and dwarven crafts from Westerhold, often incorporating the district's cultural influences.
636049259:
Cader Boneyard

The Cader Boneyard was abandoned over a hundred years ago. Located in the northernmost part of the Rego Cader, vegetation is now reclaiming the crypts and tombs that fill the place. Those buried here do not rest well, and sightings of the undead keep people away.
645307189:
Catacombs

The Catacombs of Absalom are a labyrinthine network of passageways near the Starstone Cathedral where early followers of Aroden are buried. Pilgrims can pass through one of these passages carrying a candle and receiving a coin bearing the image of one of those buried there. In recent decades, the historical value of these coins has skyrocketed, and criminals are selling counterfeit copies to unsuspecting tourists.
662138629:
Endless Cairn

The Endless Cairn is an ancient elven mausoleum located in the southern Fierani Forest of Kyonin near the border with the Tanglebriar.
667047619:
Ludovny

Ludovny is a small town north of the Hoarwood Forest in the Irriseni province of Hoarwood. The town has been the main supplier of Hoarwood's provincial capital for centuries and has managed to maintain its position using political machinations and a river of bribes.

Countess Natalka Aelena uses any means necessary to preserve Ludovny's lucrative trade status. Her two sons, Laszlo and Ludvak pull the strings of the Bitter Brotherhood, the gang of bandits who plague the trade routes from Lod and Dammartorp to Hoarwood. The countess's youngest daughter, Bozidara is also involved in Natalka's diplomatic efforts. Bozidara lives in Hoarwood, attending the endless parties of the Duchess Anelisha, whispering poisoned words and slipping bribes into the hands of key officials and merchants.

The town itself welcomes the merchant visitors with cosy inns, rousing taverns, two extravagant brothels, and a busy theater called the Brenezoi which hosts performers from the southern lands of the Inner Sea.
687658129:
Lost Temple

Located in the southern reaches of the Katapesh desert near the border with Nex, the Lost Temple was clearly once a religious site, but no one knows which deity it was devoted to.

The ordered layout suggests that it was probably dedicated to a lawful god, but beyond that the evidence is confusing. The temple has small, individual cells that suggest it may have been a monastery of Irori. Beneath it is a huge vault reminiscent of those found in temples of Abadar, while the abandoned soaring towers look more like those dedicated to the sun goddess Sarenrae. There are even rumors that the temple may have been dedicated to the dead god Aroden, though there is no evidence of this.

The site often draws adventurers of a religious bent to the site determined to identify who it was dedicated to. This constant influx of armed adventurers has kept the site relatively free of the dangerous monsters that often use abandoned ruins as lairs.
689424669:
Sedeq

Sedeq is a city in southern Qadira and the center of the nation's lucrative slave trade. It is located on the Alavah Peninsula south of the Zho Mountains, and is also known for its warm breezes and lush gardens.
725197399:
Merciful Bay

Merciful Bay is a large port town on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, located between the towns of Kozan to the north, and Golsifar to the south.
735369979:
Casnoriva

Casnoriva is a ruined star-shaped manor in the heart of the Hungry Mountains in the modern Ustalavic county of Virlych. It was founded as Ustalav's arcane college by Casnori, a Varisian wizard, in what was then called Virholt. Casnori aided Soividia Ustav at the time of the founding of the nation of Ustalav in the 24th Century AR.

Teaching at the college continued until it became one of the first targets of Tar-Baphon on his return to Golarion as a lich in the 33rd century AR. The lich threw hordes of demons from the Outer Rifts to attack the school, led by his marilith servant Kaltestrua. The attack was successful, and as of 4713 AR Kaltestrua still controlled Casnoriva while awaiting signs from Gallowspire of her master's return. Nevertheless, she still suffers ongoing resistance to her occupation from the ghosts of the Ustalavic mages she defeated centuries before. The mages are led by Mistress Qai in an endless arcane battle within the ruins.
738470309:
Lissalan Temple

The Lissalan Temple in the Wyvern Mountains is one of several temples dedicated to the rune goddess in Varisia. During Earthfall, the entrance was blocked by boulders, and for millennia, no one disturbed the location until modern cultists of the goddess discovered that it contained tokens capable of leading them to where Krune lay, awaiting awakening. In 4713 AR, pathfinders following the cultists' trail found the temple and obtained some tokens.
739311369:
Cauldheart

Cauldheart is a pit-like valley of grey stone in the Amaans peaks of the Hungry Mountains, between Mount Aremetrus and Gristleknob in the nation of Ustalav. It stretches over three miles in the shape of a human liver, and is so steep that no path leads to its depths; it is not considered scalable, and only ivy escapes from the pit.

This dark location generates rumours, including the use of Cauldheart as a gigantic witches' cauldron used by covens to perform all manner of evil: summoning foul weather and evil gods, crafting slave abominations, and corrupting the land.
754722709:
Palin's Lighthouse

Palin's Lighthouse can be found on the small Jeggare Isle in Conqueror's Bay, and is part of the Varisian city of Korvosa. It is the only structure on the island, and is presumably named after Captain Keyra Palin, a hero from the early years of Korvosa's history.
763127989:
Abken

One of the newest settlements in Varisia, the village of Abken was founded in 4668 AR as a commune by peace-loving, lower-class Korvosans. It is located on the banks of the upper Yondabakari River (before it flows into Lake Syrantula), about 45 miles northeast of Melfesh. The town is surrounded by a wooden palisade, and is fiercely protected by its inhabitants.
763255689:
Dobrova

Dobrova is a small Irriseni town on the Marbleflow River in the province of Wintercrux. As of 4713 AR it struggled under the notorious distinction of having the nation's highest taxes, thanks to its new ruler, Baron Vasyl Yelizaveta.
783846299:
Eastern Lighthouse

The Eastern Lighthouse is one of the most enduring structures in Devil's Elbow, having been built through the efforts of Yaris Neraken and being the spot from which the poor man jumped to his death. Even after decades of neglect, the exterior of this 40-foot-tall tower remains, covered in grime, yes, but still standing.
786008049:
Nibung

Nibung is a remote coffee-farming settlement in the western Kullan Dei foothills of Nagajor. Much of the hamlet has been destroyed by the warring Daughters of the Lotus and Ruby Circle gangs.
791436159:
Berus

Berus is a very prosperous settlement along the northern border of the Ustalavic county of Ardeal on the banks of the River of Scales.
798161299:
Arkona Palace

3 locations:
  • Ironworks
  • Jeggare Light
  • Old City Hall
800732119:
Wisdom's Refuge

Wisdom's Refuge is where the district's Wise Council conducts its administrative activities, focusing on education and the arts. To further their goals, many institutions dedicated to preserving history or knowledge receive reduced taxes, which are then passed on to students at the district's numerous schools and academies. Many council members have studied at the Arcanamirium, so they are aware of the dangers of magic manipulating elections and ensure that all processes adhere to the district's philosophy of fairness.
801695389:
Ash Hollow

Ash Hollow is a small mining village in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains in northeastern Nidal. It is known for being the primary supplier of coal for Pangolais, and for its annual Festival of Night's Return, held on the nearby mountain of Aghor Thal.
803073469:
Sadiyeh

Sadiyeh is a city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.
817216969:
Tar-Kazmukh

Tar-Kazmukh is the northernmost settlement in the Five Kings Mountains. It's famous for its rather impressive arcane library which not only draws dwarven wizards from all over the region, even distant Janderhoff, but also human and elf visitors. The librarians, called the Blue Warders, serve all visitors, but pay particular respect to the local Sages' Guild, whose members are dedicated to study of arcane and planar knowledge.
820382139:
Fort Serenko

Fort Serenko is an abandoned wooden fortification formerly used to defend the southern border of Rostland in Brevoy. It is located along the Shrike River, upriver from the Crooked Falls.

This fortification has stood for years, but with the growing tensions between Rostland and Issia, the soldiers and scouts stationed here have been recalled to Restov.
825906759:
Katapesh

7 locations:
  • Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie
  • House of Whips
  • Jerikal's Ales
  • Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval
  • The Scorpion's Sting
  • The Slace Block
  • Trillia's Bathhouse
828689049:
Crosspine

Located along the southeastern edge of the Uskwood, Crosspine is a village that stands out due to its distinctive practice of guiding a remarkably elevated proportion of its youth into the paths of arcane and druidic learning. Among its notable denizens are Isiem, a prominent shadowcaller, and Ascaros, a renowned sorcerer skilled in shadow magic.
833878659:
Thorn River bandit camp

The semi-permanent hideout, known as the Thorn River bandit camp lies roughly 60 feet from the Thorn River in the Narlmarches. It is set up on both sides of a narrow, meandering creek and a forest trail that runs beside it. The camp is inhabited by eleven bandits, who are responsible for raiding Oleg's Trading Post in the name of the Stag Lord.
839302129:
Boiling Maw

Boiling Maw is a rift that seems to bore into the very depths of Golarion in the south of the Vudran region of Open Bridge. Most of the Open Bridge region is covered in rifts that split the earth and disgorge hordes of aberrations, and Boiling Maw seems to be one of the most active.
839748739:
Tafinyah

Tafinyah is a small, little-known stronghold in Qadira's Tapur Forest that is home to the Hatharat, the nation's intelligence agency and diplomatic corps.
842691319:
Sallowshore

Sallowshore is a port town on the coast of Geb directly east from Thornhearth. The town appears to be sinking into the ocean, with half the buildings already partially sunken under the water. The upper floors that poke above the water are connected by shoddy wooden bridges.
861271089:
Heidmarch Manor

Home of the noted Pathfinders Canayven Heidmarch and Venture-Captain Sheila Heidmarch, Heidmarch Manor is the first Pathfinder lodge in all Varisia, serving as the Pathfinder Society's base of operations into the rest of Varisia. Heidmarch Manor is located in Magnimar, the so-called "City of Monuments", within the prosperous Alabaster District of the city's wealthy Summit area. The manor is located just north of the Triodea in the neighboring Naos District and contains all the usual amenities of a Pathfinder lodge—including some with a Varisian twist.
868346809:
The Old Light

The Old Light is the most visible landmark of the western Varisian town of Sandpoint. It is believed to have been a lighthouse during the ancient Thassilonian empire, though much of the structure has crumbled over the ages.
871582879:
Covenant Rock

Covenant Rock is a small, flat rock that sits along the northern reaches of the Flood Road. The only thing exceptional about this otherwise uninteresting rock is its use. Caravans traveling the Flood Road place here their tribute to the dragons of Raschka-Tor in exchange for safe passage through the dragons' territory. These tributes can consist of anything ranging from treasure to livestock to slaves. Regardless of what is given, it is seen as better than facing the wrath of the dragons of Raschka-Tor. Since 4710 AR, the orcs of the surrounding Whisperfall region have also started paying tributes to the dragons.

The dromaar alchemist Rzonca the Clever resides in a small hut in the shadow of the Covenant Rock and acts as the agent of the red dragons of Raschka-Tor.
879064649:
Tomb of Emperors

The Tomb of Emperors is a ruin of the ancient Jistka Imperium located in what is today the nation of Rahadoum. It is situated in the eastern desert, beyond the Uta River, and is a destination for the Pathfinders of Swordmeet Lodge in Manaket.

The Tomb of Emperors was a sprawling, colonnaded structure built at the Oasis of Xerapsion and it is known to have housed treasures made by famous Jistkan artisans. The Tomb was originally dedicated to the fifth ruler of Jistka Imperium, Inperantike Eraliskos, after her death at a decisive battle at Xeraspion—a battle that expanded the Imperium to the region east of the Uta. The nomadic tribes of the area became so impressed by the monument that a lot of nomads joined the Imperium army, forming an entire legion. Ancient Osirian invaders and their div allies looted the surface of the structure and most of its offering chambers during the era of the Night Plague but the Tekritani refused to further pillage the Tomb, leaving its lower levels and catacombs untouched.
883176249:
Mordant Spire

The island of Eysuna, located in the Steaming Sea on the southern tip of the Ironbound Archipelago, is home to several thousand aloof and alien Mordant Spire elves who are hostile towards outsiders. The island is one of the few inhabited remnants of Azlant, and the elves closely guard the secrets of the once-great continent in fear that trespassers would bring a second Earthfall upon Golarion.
896541229:
Isseld

Isseld is a minor town on the Frozen Road in Irrisen's Feyfrost province where most people earn their living from the trade on the river. The settlement has recently become known as the center of the trade in "primitive wares", mostly carved fetishes and talismans crafted by the Kellid barbarians of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords.
897336479:
Eongman Bog

Eongman Bog is the largest enclave of dokkaebi in Tian Xia, with few human residents. The settlement hosts an annual festival where dokkaebi engage in kimchi-eating competitions.
898106939:
Thanegraves

The Thanegraves, located in western Wintercrux in Irrisen, are the final resting place of the Djurstor Confederacy thanes. Two boreal ogre tribes claim the hilly stretch, which is dotted with burial mounds and tombs and largely forgotten by the area's Ulfen folk.
905108489:
Godsgate

The Godsgate is the largest gate in the outer walls of the Andoren capital of Almas. It is also the easternmost gate, and from here visitors can easily access the East Hill, Portside, and Liberty District. Ever since the People's Revolt of 4669 AR brought democracy to the nation, the doors of the Godsgate have never been closed, a source of great pride for the people of Almas.
910701079:
Guiltspur

Guiltspur is an ancient Thassilonian ruin on Varisia's northern Storval Plateau connected to the dread realm of Leng.
911623409:
Wreck of the Jenivere

4 locations:
  • Castaway Beach
  • Golden Bow
  • Tattooed Lady
  • Tears of Grog
920524239:
Gitna

Gitna is an iruxi settlement that has existed here since before Geb and Nex began their conflict.
925582719:
Uralt's Walk

Uralt's Walk is a path that the priests of Aroden built alongside Aroden's Rise so that pilgrims could take the path as a physical religious rite by climbing the hill without using the ramp. Each of the 166 steps has a word engraved on it, flanked by the Eyes of Aroden, forming a liturgy that can be read while walking.
937897479:
Valley of the Azlanti

Nel-Shevotha is an isolated, reclusive settlement in the foothills of the World's Edge Mountains, inhabited by the undead remains of colonists of the ancient Azlanti Empire who died during Earthfall. The name "Nel-Shevotha" has been lost to history, and in modern Golarion the region is called the Valley of the Azlanti or Valley of the Azlant.
946760759:
Dome of the Savored Sting

7 locations:
  • Aquaretum
  • Bent Rib Alley
  • Celwynvian Charge
  • Church of Pharasma
  • Deadeye Lodge
  • Founder's Honor
  • Seerspring Garden
966887259:
Dragon Temple

The Dragon Temple, also known as the Celestial Dragon Temple, is a retreat in the Wall of Heaven mountains on the western border of Quain that is a famous center for martial arts training in Tian Xia. It is also a temple dedicated to the god Irori. Students come from across the continent, including distant Minkai, to study under the masters of the Dragon Temple.
969201349:
Tempest

6 locations:
  • District Courthouse
  • It Sparkles!
  • Ivy District Park
  • Ivy Playhouse
  • Northman's Woodworks Galleria
  • Shrine of Shelyn
982024019:
Wreck of the Brine Demon

2 locations:
  • Scallywag
  • Windwar
985345069:
Voghul Caverns

The Voghul Caverns are winding warrens beneath the center of the Chitterwood in Isger.
993062509:
Wolfpoint

Wolfpoint is the name of an Isgeri fort located north of the Chitterwood on the Conerica River, about 25 miles southwest of the town of Saringallow. It houses approximately 300 soldiers and their officers, many of whom are veterans of the recent Goblinblood Wars. The fort itself consists of several wooden barracks surrounded by a tall log palisade. Much of the palisade was destroyed during the wars and has since been rebuilt, although many sections still show signs of fire damage and goblin blood.
993078209:
Lurching Mountain

The Lurching Mountain is a towering volcano on Raugsmauda's Reach island in the Shackles. The volcano is the primary source of the yellow-gray clouds that loom over the island. Thick fumes always pour out of its crater at the top of the mountain and roll down its sides. The resulting ash looks rotten and it is unknown what chemical composition could create such a unique and unsettling ash cloud. It is said that the lich Raugsmauda practices her foul magic on the top of the volcano.
1001612379:
Stoneclimb

Stoneclimb is a fortress along the eastern side of the Icerime Peaks in Brevoy. It is south of Skywatch and it is in the north of lands controlled by House Medvyed and from where that House lives and rules.
1002341339:
Rimeskull

Rimeskull is a mountain on the western edge of the Kodar Mountains in northwestern Varisia. Located on the banks of Lake Stormunder, it is named for the vaguely skull-shaped carving visible from miles away, the mouth of which is a large cave entrance.

The carving is a relic of ancient Thassilon, and the person depicted in the massive carving is the founder of Thassilon itself: Emperor Xin. During the time of this ancient empire, runelords commonly carved their faces into the mountainsides that faced their capital cities. The carving of Xin, however, faces the Runeforge, a place of great importance shared by all of the runelords.

At the mountain's western base, just off the lake, lies a ring of seven ten-foot-tall, open-mouthed stone heads angled toward each other known as the Sihedron Circle. This Thassilonian ruin was recently discovered to be a magical entrance to the Runeforge.

The territorial white dragon Arkrhyst makes his home on Rimeskull and endangers any expedition there.
1005798079:
Fort Rannick

Fort Rannick is a remote fortress in the Valley of Broken Trees of central Varisia. Established by the mayor of Turtleback Ferry to help protect river-trading interests from the region's ogres, its rangers—the Order of the Black Arrows—defended the river pass for more than four decades before being routed by ogres and ogrekin in 4707 AR.
1021227919:
Pediment Building

5 locations:
  • Cenotaph
  • Founder's Archive and Museum of Ages
  • Indros cul Vydrarch
  • Osprey Club
  • Usher's Hall
1024124749:
Trunau

Trunau is a small human farming community located just south of the old Hordeline in the Hold of Belkzen. When the orcs breached this defensive border in 4515 AR, the citizens of the town refused to abandon their land, unlike most of the farmers in the surrounding region. They built wooden palisades and ramparts, surrounding the defenses with pits and stakes, and defended the walls of their town with great ferocity. The orc invaders eventually gave up besieging the town and simply started circumventing it. Those few orcs who trade with the inhabitants began naming the settlement Manhome. The inhabitants of this town are called 'Trunauans'.

Every able-bodied citizen of Trunau is tasked with defending the town at a moment's notice and is grimly aware that the town is the lone human outpost in a savage land. Children on their twelfth birthday are presented with a hopeknife and shown precisely which arteries to cut should they or their families be taken alive by the orc hordes.
1033907369:
Grayhaven

Grayhaven is a settlement built by House Garess in the Golushkin Mountains in Western Brevoy.
1034726879:
Kukgukmol

Kukgukmol is a small village on the Cannibal Isles in the Shackles. Kukgukmol has been at war with the neighboring village of Heggapnod for years. The two villages rapidly lose men, and they will surely obliterate one another if nobody steps in to stop them. The Blood Queen enjoys this bloodshed and watches the weekly massacres through its kuru surrogates in each village.
1034733679:
Magrevore

Magrevore is an enormous roiling magma pit located in the Darklands deep beneath the Qadiran city of Khoka. A portal at the heart of Magrevore leads to the depths of Malebolge, the sixth layer of Hell.

Magrevore is guarded by a powerful nessari named Mardehzuk, a servant of the archdevil Moloch, who controls a far-reaching network of hryngar slavers that funnels a constant stream of slaves to be sacrificed in Moloch's name.
1036010469:
Edammera's Folly

Edammera's Folly is a tall spire locked behind large rune-scribed steel doors. It is one of the Towers of the Fiendlorn constructed to serve the experiments toward immortality by Mesandroth Fiendlorn, but was sealed after a planar rift to the Netherworld opened in its base in -398 AR and flooded the tower with creatures of shadow.
1041987839:
Obelisk of Wonders

The Obelisk of Wonders is a mysterious ruined monument on the island of Kepre Dua in the Shackles. It is carefully guarded by the elven witch Kaala Raingold, who believes she receives revelations from it.
1049329799:
Franax

Franax, a port town located on the Lake of Mists and Veils, was once the centre of Iobaria's shipbuilding and repair thanks to its advantageous geography. Located on a series of bays protected by three islands, Franax's port eventually spread to cover the islands. However, much of the town now lies in ruins after a fire consumed 80% of the town in 4651 AR. The fire was started in an ill-advised attempt to contain the "plague of boils and bone-aches" that afflicted Franax, but ultimately led to an exodus of its shipbuilding industry to Orlov. Now those few who remain in the city are caught in a conflict between the zealous druids of the Glacircle and local fisherman Niath Koyra.
1061951769:
Volod

Volod is a small Iobarian town in the Norinor built in 4400 AR to replace the former logging town of Hroran. Located on the Noyrus River, the town has been dominated by logging and woodcrafting trades for much of its history. As of 4710 AR, strange events had recently been occurring around Volod, and the town's druids spoke of new deities in the highlands and Norinor who demanded worship.
1067958709:
Olfden

Olfden is a large Andoren town located in the nation's Darkmoon Vale region, halfway between Falcon's Hollow and Oregent, and only a few miles away from Adamas, headquarters of the Diamond Regiment.
1070284919:
Raschka-Tor

Raschka-Tor is the lair of the most fearsome family of red dragons in the entire Hold of Belkzen. The family consists of four red dragons, two still only wyrmlings. They were one of the greatest threats to anyone traveling across northern Belkzen until the idea of leaving them tribute at Covenant Rock caught on. The dragons live in a mountainside cave known as Raschka-Tor which is orc for fire mountain. Rumour has it that their lair is in fact just the entrance to a vast city carved within the mountain itself. However, due to the dragons' dislike of nosy adventurers, no one has yet been able to verify these rumours.
1073843979:
Sharpened Axe

The Sharpened Axe of Urgir in the Hold of Belkzen is a fighting school located on the southeastern side of the city's fourth level.
1081718229:
Crevasse Fields

The Crevasse Fields are a glacial fissure in northern Irrisen, avoided by all travelers. It stretches 25 miles wide and 75 miles long, a expanse of pure ice and cliffs. No one would normally venture into the area, but powerful magic can be detected there, suggesting some kind of artifact is responsible for the region's instability and rumors abound that it was left by the gods, or perhaps by Baba Yaga, to plunge Irrisen into an eternal winter. Because of this, many adventurers have explored the region, and while some return with tales of treasure, none have come back with an answer to the mystery of the ice's power. To make matters worse, a traveler must also contend with the Crevasse Fields' inhabitants: will-o'-wisps, at least three linnorms, and a wendigo said to devour souls.
1087708459:
Cebarinne Estate

4 locations:
  • Alabaster Academy
  • Long Roads Coffeehouse
  • Newt Market
  • Nightways Gate
1112004359:
Shadefields

The Shadefields are a battleground marked by a simple stone cairn that commemorates one of the fiercest battles ever fought between the orcs and the Shoanti of Varisia's Storval Plateau.
1126340029:
Javelin Gallery

The Javelin Gallery is the base of operations for the Guild of Spears, composed of veteran guards who have joined as mercenaries. The structure was a second-millennium manor house converted into a defensive fortification and boasts enough forges to equip an entire army if needed. The Guild has contracts with various mercenaries in the city and they are either highly respected or feared, depending on who sees them.
1144795489:
Cyremium

Cyremium is a small, isolated fishing town on the Inner Sea coast, just east of the southern tip of the Cape of Hope on the Missa Shelf. Now located within the Arthfell region of Andoran, the town once was the site of a Taldan fortress that was abandoned soon after the end of the Even-Tongued Conquest of 4081 AR. Local settlers began dismantling the four watchtowers to build their homes as soon as the soldiers left, although the outer, sun-bleached walls still remain.
1152090619:
Black Tower

The Black Tower is a 400-foot-tall columnar tower of black stone located at the center of its namesake island in the western Shackles. Only 30 feet in diameter, it is said to resemble a large pin plunged into the earth.
1159483829:
Veshtak

Veshtak is a small town on the Marbleflow River in the Irriseni province of Wintercrux. The town is in a strategic location where the merchants load their cargo onto ships traveling the river north.

Veshtak won the right to govern itself a generation ago, using bribes and political connections. The town exploits its position by exacting high tariffs, soliciting bribes, and demanding a share of traded goods. As of 4713 AR the Cold Sisters kept an eye on the greedy Ulfen townsfolk of Veshtak, and a number of former mayors, dock wardens, and warehouse managers hung in cages in the town's main square.

Veshtak's mayor as of 4713 AR was the greedy Vidlof Haradsson. Few know that the dockworker Marina Gnetsky is actually Vidlof's boss. When the Cold Sisters come to town, Marina made sure that Vidlof would be punished instead of her.
1163115629:
Lucky Bones

7 locations:
  • Holding House
  • Odde & Daughter Herbs
  • Sallix Salt Works
  • Salt Gate
  • Salt Market
  • Sweet Tooth
  • Tooth and Nail
1184913829:
Gold Canyon

Gold Canyon is not a natural canyon but rather the remains of an ancient strip mine used in Taldor's early history. It is located in Meratt County within Tandak Prefecture, east of Cassomir. The ravine is 80 feet wide, 30 feet deep, and runs for almost 10 miles in total, and its bottom is filled with stagnant water.

It once had a stone bridge crossing it, but this collapsed decades ago during a small earthquake. While it is the responsibility of Baron Telus, he has shown no interest in repairing the structure. Locals on foot can still cross the canyon relatively easily due to steps cut into the canyon's already stepped sides, but this route is impassable for horses and wagons.

Gold Canyon once boosted a rich vein of gold and quartz that was mined out over 4,000 years ago, and most of the metal was used to mint early Taldan currency. The canyon is thus a minor holy site of Abadar, and local worshippers make small pilgrimages to the site. Such followers search the bottom of the canyon for any remaining gold, taking even a small flake of it as a sign of Abadar's favour. Unbeknownst to these pilgrims, the canyon's floor is also home to a dangerous snake-like creature known as a Goldpebble.
1192533149:
Thrylheim

Thrylheim is the ruined city of the frost giant nation that once controlled much of the Fog Peaks. It was destroyed in a devastating war with the rime dragons of the mountains and is now largely in ruin. Underground portions of the city are still said to exist, and hold the frost giant sword Eldavar, used by the last konig of Thrylhim, Virtosk, to kill the elder rime dragon Keljanir.

Gao Bie

Gao Bie (pronounced gau-bier), also spelled Gau Bie, is a large town in the Sun Shogunate of Songbai that serves as a waystation for land and river trade routes. Its name is derived from the Tien phrase for "goodbye".

During peacetime Gao Bie hosts traders and furloughed soldiers, and the town's lack of security despite its strategic location also draws criminals and adventurers.
1211914449:
Jernashall

Jernashall was the preeminent city of the dwarven kingdom of Tar Khadurrm, and was located in the southern reaches of the Five Kings Range.
1217593189:
Sascha

Sascha, once called Ermalin, is a small Irriseni town in the Verge with a troubled past.
1219292399:
Saruhk

Saruhk is a small religious community and temple complex located in the northwestern jungles of the Mwangi Expanse. It is protected from the outside world by Anwigasi, the divine herald of the empyreal lord Winlas.
1246614819:
Haugin's Ear

The Isgeri town of Haugin's Ear is situated on the Keld River south of the Chitterwood, right on the border with Cheliax.
1260833539:
Scar of the Spider

The Scar of the Spider is a small, fungi-encrusted vale in the southwestern Felldales of Numeria, just north of the West Sellen River. Generally avoided by everyone, this valley of gray stone spears is said to be the home of a steel arachnid that strikes terror into even the stoutest warriors. Other legends tell of a glass coffin located somewhere in the vale, wherein writhes a dancing and whistling enigma.
1263063139:
Kortos Watercrafts

The Kortos Watercrafts is a shipyard that creates boats and small vessels using the finest quality wood on the island. They are owned by the Kortos Consortium, which uses the vessels to navigate the region's waterways.
1278289059:
Alkenstar City

Alkenstar City is the capital city of the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar and houses the whole of the Duchy's governmental infrastructure. The city is located on top of the Hellfallen Cliffs, where the Ustradi River falls seven hundred feet down Alken Falls. Monumental works of engineering and architecture, including the Bridge of the Gods, connect the city to the vassal communities of Martel and Dongun Hold. As a result of the urban development of the Duchy, many incorrectly consider these vassals mere districts of Alkenstar City proper, rather than distinct communities in their own right.

Alkenstar City is also the nation's center of industry, housing the Duchy's infamous Gunworks, the source of mass-produced firearms used both for the protection of the city-state and for trade throughout the Inner Sea region. As a result of the industrial advancement of Alkenstar, a constant cloud of white steam and sooty smoke hangs in a haze over the city, generated by the gears and pistons of countless factories operating around the clock.

To the west of the city are the Karggat Hills, a rocky region dotted with mines and factories.

Hajoth Hakados

The small trade city of Hajoth Hakados can be found on the Seven Tears River and was often the first settlement Mendevian Crusaders visited in Numeria on their way north to the Worldwound before its closure. Because of this, it seemed more like a city in the nearby River Kingdoms than a true Numerian settlement.
1308863619:
Goka

Goka (pronounced go-kar) is a free city-state on the western coast of Tian Xia. Formerly part of Imperial Lung Wa, it broke free when the empire collapsed and is now enjoying a golden age of trade and prosperity. Goka is a vital port city that rivals Absalom and Katapesh in size and diversity, and serves as a gateway to Tian Xia for travelers from the west.
1312832729:
Arboretum Arcanis

The Arboretum Arcanis once contained a wonderland of exotic fruit and blossoms from beyond Golarion. It was housed in a magical crystal dome in the Precipice Quarter in Absalom.

The magic that protected and nurtured the flora was placed by Beldrin, but these wavered and collapsed when the earthquake of 4698 AR struck the district. The magical cloud that hovered overhead, designed to provide nutrient-rich rain, became corrupted. Now the purple-black cloud spills caustic gray and yellow rain on the plants below. The plants that once thrived either withered away or evolved into horrific versions of themselves, while new abominations of nature have since sprung into existence.
1330580299:
Zaplava

Zaplava is a little village on Rimeflow River just before reaching Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Wintercrux. Few travelers stop at the village, inhabited by as many as seven intermarried families. The miserable superstitious peasants live in fear of the dreaded Frozen Fog to the east. The fog's icy tendrils and the strange horrors that sometimes wander close to the village make the frightened peasants hide in their stone hovels. Arko Arkosson is the mayor of Zaplava.

Todora Navanya, a renowned adventurer in Avistan, comes from this tiny village.

1345139219:
Banukmaud

The village of Banukmaud is the second-largest Ghol-Gan ruin in the Cannibal Isles. It is inhabited by the cannibalistic humanoids known as the kuru.
1358668789:
Artume

Artume is a small kingdom amid hills and fertile plains within the River Kingdoms. Artume is located on a western branch of the Sellen River, not far from the cities of Daggermark, Gralton, and Sevenarches. The region boasts high-quality livestock, especially horses, as well as leather goods and stone. The kingdom welcomes adventurers, who are frequently requested to deal with bandits and nagas. The nation has recently experienced a rebellion, resulting in the overthrow of Regent Waike in favour of Edryd Artume and the restoration of the monarchy.
1367974429:
District Courthouse

The District Courthouse in The Coins is located in the western part of the district, just outside the Grand Bazaar. It is in this building that district justice should be administered, but although several corrupt judges have been removed in recent years, there are always people willing to reduce sentences or declare innocent those whose wallets weigh more heavily on them.
1373822459:
Eldentre

The town of Eldentre can be found in the south-western corner of the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, on the banks of the Thundering River. The town is famous for the Lightning Oak, and the weapons that are made using the electrical energy from this tree.
1380883389:
Well of Tainted Virtue

The Well of Tainted Virtue is a temple of Lissala in the Stony Mountains that survived the destruction caused by Earthfall. The temple has halls dedicated to each school of magic practiced in the time of Thassilon. A modern cult of the goddess protected the site, and the Aspis Consortium was able to make contact with them to reach an agreement in which they would open a backdoor to the Hao Jin Tapestry, which their enemies in the Pathfinder Society had obtained. In 4712 AR, a team of pathfinders raided the temple, eliminating their opponents and using the site to travel between Absalom and Varisia.
1390420489:
Vaneo Arvanxi

Vaneo Arvanxi was a vast estate in the Rego Sacero of Westcrown that as of 4709 AR served as the home of Lord-Mayor Aberian Arvanxi, into which he had poured such immense wealth that it had earned the local nickname of Aberian's Folly.

Verennie's Lair

Verennie's Lair is an abandoned two-story house with a garden in Rego Cader, chosen by the traitor Ara Verennie of the Hellknights. The place was imbued with her malevolent essence, causing strange effects in the house.
1400279509:
Dawnton

Dawnton is a small Mendevian village located on the plains between the Egelsee River and the western Estrovian Forest.

House of Sky

The House of Sky is an aerie high in the Five Kings Range that is the home of the giant owls of the Fierani Forest. These intelligent creatures have been allied with the elves of Kyonin for millennia, but are quite mistrustful of any other humanoid who makes the dangerous trek to their home. They hate Treerazer and his demonic minions, and can be summoned by the rulers of Kyonin in times of need via enchanted whistles. They are led by Winglord Kreiagh, who oversees a council of owls who frequently disagree with one another, but whose every word is taken to heart. Travel to the House of Sky is quite dangerous on foot, and is most easily accomplished by flight.

Zekrotska

Zekrotska is a logging hamlet on the western edge of the Hoarwood Forest in Irrisen.
1442174849:
Demgazi

Demgazi is a small Taldan town located on the Jalrune River in Sophra Prefecture, on the border between Taldor and Qadira, south of the Border Wood. It is also across the Wood from the town of Heldren, and contributes inexperienced fighters to the High Sentinels.
1448078299:
Mammoth Graveyard

The Mammoth Graveyard is a mountain valley in the eastern foothills of the northern Tusk Mountains, where mammoths near the end of their lives travel to die. The valley has grown into an intelligent genius loci, guiding mammoths to itself while confounding travellers, who only find it by accident and never twice. The formation of the Worldwound, however, corrupted the Mammoth Graveyard, and mammoths ceased coming there; it became home to bone golems and a massive flesh colossus made from rotting mammoth corpses.
1448619949:
Farsouth Mine

The Farsouth Mine is a site in the Mwangi Expanse section of the Bandu Hills close to the border with Sargava. It was founded in 4164 AR after gold was discovered here by dwarves.
1451638959:
Janderhoff

Janderhoff is a dwarven Sky Citadel and settlement in southeastern Varisia's Mindspin Mountains, north of Korvosa and south of the Cinderlands.
1458383269:
Jerikal's Ales

Jerikal's Ales is a cantina and brewery located in the Docks of Katapesh city and is uniquely run by a cleric of Shelyn called Jerikal Ashworth who has picked brewing as his chosen medium. The interior is dedicated to Shelyn: filled with beautiful statuary, fresh flowers and other beautifications that his patrons can enjoy along with his unique brews. Despite being run by a worshipper of Shelyn, it is like many traditional breweries, a site of worship for followers of Cayden Cailean, the Drunken Hero; this mini congregation is led by Tomlin Orlof who gets along well with Jerikal. Jerikal's Ales is renowned for it unique "priest in a bottle" brews which are combination of magical potions and alcoholic beverages. Jerikal is also a source of endless rumours especially adventure related ones, in his adventuring days he even acquired a map leading to a catacomb beneath Pale Mountain filled with treasure.
1469458489:
Corna Barracks

The Corna Barracks served the city's dottari in their operations throughout the district, though during the anarchy of 4710 AR it was temporarily abandoned and taken over by Hellknights who had deserted their posts. The Hellknights had allied themselves with the Council of Thieves, who promised to restore law and order to the city when they seized power.
1473980069:
Gray Falls Lodge

Just west of the Embeth Forest, a gray and weather-worn gristmill leans wearily over a trickling stream that slowly feeds into one of the dozens of winding tributaries of the Sellen River. The gristmill belongs to Sayrin Firewyne, a gnome with a romanticized interest in her fey roots who specializes in the lost knowledge of the First World.

The Gristmill serves as the front for the unlikely venture-captain's Pathfinder lodge, called the Gray Falls Lodge. It also serves as a sanctuary for a handful of local fey, in addition to being the permanent residence of a small band of pixies who nest in the rafters, and a nixie who resides in the stream.

The Gristmill contains an observatory and is powered by a secret magical contraption that resides behind a waterfall near the cottage.
1479204379:
Black Edifice

The Black Edifice was a Thrallkeepers complex near the Fenwall Mountains where sorcerers summoned all manner of monsters from other planes to serve the Runelords. After Earthfall, the order abandoned the building, and their summoned creatures, including the powerful Koth'Vaul, waited patiently for millennia until, in 4713 AR, the runes binding him began to degrade. A ghaele noticed this and went to fight the demon, but neither could kill the other. A group of pathfinders explored the location and rescued the ghaele, though Koth'Vaul escaped into the Outer Rifts.
1480688699:
Bosco

Bosco is a town on the eastern coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom, just southeast of the Tyrant's Grasp.

Duskwarden Guildhouse

Within are a few offices for ranking officials of the group, barracks for new recruits (or any member with no other place to sleep), workshops, and a modest store of equipment.

Wolf's Ear

The town of Wolf's Ear is located in western Varisia where the Lampblack River empties into Ember Lake. It gains its name from the unusually large population of lycanthropes who once called the community home.
1511705929:
Satravah

Satravah, an Ustalavic village deep in the heart of Ulcazar, is better known as the Vale of Honorless Graves—site of countless secret burials in the Shining Crusade against Tar-Baphon.
1520825659:
Moonscream Glade

Circa 4713 AR, when the Worldwound was still open, Moonscream Glade—formerly known as Moonglade—was a small town of fiendish werewolves.
1525137009:
Pyre Crest

Pyre Crest is a volcano in the area of Brazen Peaks that remains uninhabited by the kholo that otherwise litter the range. Not even scrubs grow on the scorched sides of Pyre Crest though the eruption that covered the sides of the mountain in lava occurred centuries ago. Blackened tracks of hardened magma cover the surface of the mountain like veins.
1532429509:
Marble Sphinx

The titanic statue of the Marble Sphinx in the city of Katapesh is 120 feet tall. It depicts a sitting creature with the body of a lioness and the head of a long-haired female human with sharp tusks jutting up from the corners of her mouth. The statue is not made from marble, but rather from sandstone.

When the first settlers arrived to Katapesh, they discovered this enormous statue of a sphinx sitting as if it was guarding the valley. Those settlers built the ancient Golden City at this site in 2218 AR.
1533374269:
Devi

The Devi is a 200-foot-tall statue located on an island in the Kalyan River, near where it becomes Sona Lake, in the Vudran region of Golden Basin.

The Devi depicts the legendary psychic Vanitapati, who defeated the Spawn of Rovagug known as Kothogaz, the Dance of Disharmony, at this site. The Devi is both a site of pilgrimage and potent psychic nexus.
1534655749:
Walking Man

The Walking Man is a 30-foot-tall tree-like construct sculpted to resemble a human man. It consists of at least four different trees bound together with ivy and other vines and covered in herbs and flowers. The flowers include amaranth, bluebell, buttercup, celandine, clover, creeping bellflower, daffodil, dragon's teeth, eyebright, goldenrod, hawksbeard, hyacinth, iris, knotweed, lily, mouse-ear, mudwort, nightshade, orchid, shepherd's purse, and tansy. It was most likely created by elves, but no one remembers when or how it was made.

Many have climbed inside of the construct's hulking body through an entrance in its shoulder and written notes. One such note, from Variel Morgethai, states that the burial mound Erithiel's Hall may somehow be connected to The Walking Man. In this room are panels that can act as controls for the construct, which were tested by Varian Jeggare. On midsummer each year, it takes one step towards Erages.

The druids have tried to communicate with the Walking Man to asks for its help to fight the Whispering Tyrant but the voice from within the construct was unintelligible. The elves have asked the Wildwood Lodge of the Verduran Forest for assistance in making their pleas hear by the ancient being.
1547042339:
Hawah

Hawah is a city in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.
1552426319:
Hostel Sepet

The Hostel Sepet is a keep at the top of Sepet Pass on the trade route between El-Fatar and Ipeq in Osirion. It is built on top of an ancient Osirian fortifications on the eastern edge of the saddle of the mountain. It is surrounded by thick stone walls that rise up several stories. The ruined keep has been sealed up tightly, with only one heavily fortified gate and the narrowest of windows. The inside of the keep is a paradise of fountains and flowers. Hostel Sepet is the absolutely safe harbor for the caravans perilously journeying across the Brazen Peaks with kholo raiders and dangerous wildlife plaguing the journey the entire way.

Ganagsau

Ganagsau is the largest set of Ghol-Gan ruins in the Cannibal Isles of the Shackles. It is also the location of their so-called deity, the Blood Queen. The Blood Queen is actually a powerful outsider of astounding power and evil who has transformed the kuru—people who were forced onto the islands—into primitive monsters.

The Blood Queen's current high priest is Bukrugsor. He serves in the Sanguine Cathedral as the intermediate between the kuru and their "goddess", making demands that include human sacrifices and other acts of repugnant devotion.
1581368589:
Bailey's Perch

Bailey's Perch is a small village on the shore of Lake Encarthan in Druma, far enough north not to be on the Gulf of Accord. It's a quiet, simple fishing community that recently caught an undead fish, which is the talk of the town.
