1594923659:
Nantambu

Nantambu, also called the Song-Wind City, is a colourful, cheerful city-state in the western Mwangi Expanse. A haven of peace, learning, and culture within the Expanse, it is home to the Magaambya, one of the oldest, if not the oldest arcane schools on Golarion.
1602529049:
Sech Nevali

Sech Nevali is a relic from the Thassilonian realm of Haruka. Also called the Hanging Monastery, it is a vast stone temple complex built upon a very thick metal platform suspended over a mile-deep chasm by three immense chains attached to three nearby mountain peaks. A fourth chain failed centuries ago, making the monastery sway disconcertingly in strong winds.
1604046149:
Rivercroft

1621250749:
Adamas

Adamas, also known as The Gleaming Tower, is a white marble tower a few miles from Olfden that rises out of the ground at the base of the Elberwick Rise.
1621871179:
Golden Oasis

The last remaining oasis that once gave the city of Katapesh its ancient name of the Golden City, the Golden Oasis is still a bastion of lush beauty. Once Katapesh had several such oases but after the Year of Scouring Winds the Golden Oasis was the only one that remained. It is feed by the River Scorpius and is surrounded by date palms and an area of quiet inns, homes and small shops, Golden Oasis is one of the first areas a lot of traders (those who enter via the Dawn Gate) see. Legend says that those who sit by the fish-filled central pool are sometimes granted visions of divine inspiration as a result many pilgrims can be found sat beneath the palms that surround the central oasis meditating. Some pilgrims leave satisfied, some just leave, others in their desperation resort to eating local spirit moss. This strange plant tastes disgusting but allows those who eat it to commune with the spirits of the dead and may be responsible for the reputation of the Golden Oasis.
1622082609:
Ravenous Sphinx

The Ravenous Sphinx is a huge statue in the shape of a crouching jet-black lion topped by the snarling head of a fantastic beast. It is located in the Parched Dunes of Osirion, in the shadow of the Brazen Peaks, partially buried in the desert's sands. It houses three large rooms, with a connecting central antechamber.
1633751599:
Naldak's Point

Naldak's Point is an abandoned dwarven town near Dalun in Irrisen.
1634644929:
Orost

Orost is a trade town of increasing importance in Iobaria. Founded in 780 AR, Orost is the only safe stop on the roads between Mirnbay and Orlov, and thus a site of great importance to the various factions vying for control of Iobaria. Envoys and diplomats from Mirnbay, Orlov, Kridorn, and Veka frequent the town alongside those seeking goods, and local law enforcement is forced to protect local citizens from the intrigue and battles these visitors bring.

At the centre of Orost stands a massive wood and stone building that houses the current komar. The temple is a human construct built over a massive, ancient stone temple. The mosaics on the floor of this temple feature stars, and while the town and its fortifications have taken on a rough star shape over the years, it is from these mosaics that Orost gets its name.
1636957769:
Ogrekin Hall

Ogrekin is a transporting business located in the Docks district of Absalom. Ogrekin can move anything that needs moving anywhere within the city. Its characteristically strong employees cooperate with the Pilot House and Harbormaster's Grange to get to ships first and offer to move their goods quickly and securely. Its base of operations is a simple building called Ogrekin Hall, distinguished by two disturbing life-sized ogrekin sculptures that flank its main entrance.

Ogrekin is owned by the Second Harbormaster, Ptarnex Dexarion, who also owns the city's Red Drake Warehouses. It is managed by his second-in-command and labormaster Feldreth Noor, and its employees work with the city's Pilot's House and Harbormaster's Grange.
1669004249:
Promise

Promise, the capital and only settlement of any significance on the island nation of Hermea in the Steaming Sea, is located in the island's southern corner.
1677434759:
Deadbridge

Deadbridge is the capital of the Protectorate of the Black Marquis in the River Kingdoms.
1706163209:
Xamne

The Iblydan city of Xamne is the capital city of the city-state of Pol-Xamne located on the island of Xaktilos.
1739534369:
Port Valen

Port Valen is an Ulfen colony on the Scarred Shores in northeastern Arcadia. It was founded by Linnorm King Ulvass of Kalsgard in -473 AR, who travelled to Arcadia after hearing the call of Valenhall, a place of unending revelry and glory.

Until recently, in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings and the Inner Sea region as a whole, the colony has only been known through stories, which refer to it as Valenhall and paint it as the final spiritual destination for powerful lords and brave warriors, who attempt the lengthy and dangerous journey to the colony at the end of their lives, though the truth is much less glamorous.
1740632869:
Rift of Niltak

The Rift of Niltak is a mist-shrouded canyon found in the Red Mountains of north-eastern Varisia. No one knows what the origins of the rift are, but all agree that it is filled with all manner of strange flora and fauna. Gigantic insects and centipede horrors can be seen crawling over plants found in no other place on Golarion, and the echoes of shrieking bat-like creatures reverberate off the glen's walls.

An accurate description of the rift has not come to light, as those who enter either never return, go insane, or kill themselves shortly after emerging from it.
1749693529:
Tazion

Tazion is an ancient Azlanti ruin located in the southern Mwangi Jungle of the Mwangi Expanse.
1752477269:
Lost Fortress of Mekshir

The Lost Fortress of Mekshir guarded the trade routes from Osirion to its province of Thuvia until it was buried in a sandstorm during the khamsin season of -841 AR. The fortress is rumored to contain treasures including an armory of magical weapons and several months of payroll, but is said to be guarded by the undead.
1758342299:
Prada Hanam

Prada Hanam is a small port city on the eastern coast of the nation of Jalmeray. The settlement's defining feature is the Murmur Dome of Prada Hanam, an enormous, multi-building structure that suddenly appeared in the town centuries ago.
1762225679:
Ostenso

Ostenso is a major port city on the southern coast of Cheliax, just east of Cape Erebus, and is the easternmost city in the country. Ostenso is currently the home port of the Chelaxian Navy with grand, spacious docks. The naval works here are the largest on the continent, making up a quarter of the city that sprawls over the water. Another quarter of the city stands upon a line of stones known as Custodisce Break, which stretches half a mile into the sea and forms the natural harbour.
1764227579:
Ñarula

Ñarula is a city in the south-west of Xopatl that once thrived on logging the nearby Manzinago Forest, but has been forced to adapt to the ever-growing danger of the Deepweave infestation.

Ñarula once grew rich from exporting timber across Xopatl and to the neighbouring kingdom of Nalmeras, but the forest has since grown dangerous from the Deepweave infestation of carnivorous plants that devour any who enter it. The people of Ñarula have taken to ranching by using arcane secrets shared by the city of Jolizpan. Ñarula's citizens have created their own arcane ley lines near their ranches which has resulted in huge livestock, but some locals fear that because of their prodigious size ranchers can no longer control these animals.
1764865879:
Peacebound Isle

Peacebound Isle is the rough Common translation of the name of an island in the western outer reaches of the Eye of Abendego. It is a former site of the ancient cyclopean empire of Ghol-Gan, and was the home of the Peacebound Seers.
1766551439:
Citadel Krane

Citadel Krane is the fortress-headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Pyre, and can be found south of the Chelaxian city of Ostenso on Cape Erebus.

The seaside fortress is built into the rising shore cliffs around a natural harbor, overlooking both nearby land and sea. Citadel Krane is the only Hellknight citadel that hosts its own fleet of warships. Its fleet consists of nearly three-dozen black-sailed heavy ships shod in dark metal and crewed by lightly armored Hellknights.

The citadel hosts the Godless Gallery, a museum of unholy artifacts and cultic writings that either defy destruction or hold the potential to be turned against their blasphemous creators. Priestly signifers protect the library, granting access only to the order's lictor, master of blades, and paravicar. The order's signifers also maintain the "undoctrination halls" within the fortress's bowels, where they liberate the heretics or savages from the bonds of their false beliefs.
1792215709:
Whispertruth

Whispertruth is a small settlement of runaways located in the Vergan Forest of Razmiran. Paranoid of being discovered, they do everything they can to keep the location of their town a secret from the government.
1800479999:
Broken Shore

The Broken Shore is a mile-wide sea of hard black glass and sand that ripples out in concentric circle patterns located in the Algid Wastes, on the amorphous border between the northern Hold of Belkzen and the Realm of the Mammoth Lords. On its edges stand statues of forgotten gods, and it is avoided by both the Kellid tribes of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords and the orcs of Belkzen, who know it to be haunted.
1800832979:
Bloodcove

The city-state of Bloodcove is a decadent haven of crime located where the Vanji River meets the Fever Sea on the edge of the vast Mwangi Expanse.
1816237629:
Mount Tsukiyo

Mount Tsukiyo is the tallest mountain in Zi Ha and one of the tallest on the continent of Tian Xia. Located in the Kelsang Mountains and named for the moon deity Tsukiyo, it attracts pilgrims who travel the month-long Tsukiyo Trek in pursuit of guidance from the sky dragon Shei Long, as well as adventure-seeking mountain climbers who race to its summit during the When Earth Touches the Sky Festival.
1818464159:
Westcrown

1832166449:
Galizhur

Galizhur is a largely dwarven mining settlement on the southern coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom.
1836841219:
Chitterhome

Chitterhome is the largest ratfolk settlement in Numeria and serves as a de facto capital to the ysoki ethnicity of Numerian Tinkerers. It is located in the Sellen Hills region, about 50 miles east of Scrapwall. The settlement itself is a square mile in size and consists of natural caverns that have been further excavated.

Chitterhome contains three, and possibly more, carefully guarded entrances to Nar-Voth.

Outsiders are not allowed in Chitterhome, but its residents trade throughout eastern Numeria, particularly Chesed, Hajoth Hakados, and along the Sellen River.
1850872729:
Hell Harbor

Hell Harbor is the largest settlement on the island of Devil's Arches, in the Shackles. It is ruled by the pirate lord Arronax Endymion, a member of the Pirate Council. Endymion is a fan of traditional Chelaxian opera, and the town boasts the Shackles' only opera house, the Three-Horned Hall. It is not clear whether the other residents share his passion for the arts, but those seeking the pirate lord's favour make a point of sitting through performances.

The city takes its name from the numerous imps which live in the area. Endymion is a mutineer from the Chelaxian Navy, and is worried that some of the imps may be Chelaxian spies. As a result, he has recently started trying to eradicate them. Ethnic Chelaxians (apart from members of Endymion's own squadron, the Devils' Own) are not welcome in the town, for the same reason. Any who arouse Endymion's suspicions are thrown into the city's prison, the Wretched Furnace.

Pirates established the port town of Hell Harbor in the early years of the Shackles, as the natural harbor is deep and easy to defend. They used limestone from ancient quarries and Ghol-Gan ruins to build tall watchtowers flanking its narrow harbor entrance. The Faded Seahorse is a tavern renowned for its cyclops bouncer Eriboe.
1852307719:
Bitter Hollow

Bitter Hollow is a tiny thorp on the banks of Lake Coal in central Varisia. It is home to only a small number of hunters and trappers, as Lake Coal has very poor fishing. The Gator's Nest, Bitter Hollow's only trading post, is located in the center of town. The founders of Bitter Hollow must have had some foresight, as they built the wood and reed walkway known as the Wicker Walk to encourage trade with gnomes of the nearby Sanos Forest. Although trade with the gnomes is not regular, one can find interesting and unusual items for sale at the Gator's Nest from time to time.
1885128099:
Footprint of the Lost Saint

The Footprint of the Lost Saint is a massive cenote located in the midst of the arid grasslands south of the Mildanesi Mountains in Xopatl. The Footprint of the Lost Saint is believed to have been caused by the death of one of Xopatl's hero gods. Very few who venture to the Footprint of the Lost Saint ever return; those who do tell tales of enormous creatures made of living crystal, and claim the cenote hides a link to the Darklands. Some say the Darklands vault below the Footprint is abandoned, but others claim it is some sort of prison.
1901326139:
Publican House

5 locations:

  • Cypher Lodge
  • Fish Bowl
  • Gold Goblin

1931660339:
Steps of the Agate King

The Steps of the Agate King is a massive pyramid located within the depths of the Sparadyn Jungle in central Katapesh. The pyramid lists gently to the west having sunk into the earth. The pyramid is made of large blocks streaked with red and golden agate that make it dazzling to look upon.

It is generally accepted by all the citizens of Katapesh, despite a lack of scholarly evidence, that the Steps of the Agate King bar the way to the underground tomb of the legendary Agate King. People also accept that he took all the trappings of life with him when he was buried, and that somewhere buried beneath is a king's fortune.

While there is an underground section to the site, it lies completely empty, perhaps stripped bare of its former wealth, or perhaps never having any in the first place. Regardless, every year a few hardy souls visit the Steps of the Agate King desperately looking for the rumoured treasure-filled tomb layer.
1941878759:
Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall

Morilla Palace is a beautiful residence with magnificent gardens, incredible paintings and sculptures, and traps everywhere, capable of killing in ways one can't even imagine. It's one of the places most avoided by thieves, as the members of House Morilla are adept at hundreds of methods of killing, and the Lotus Guard patrols the area severely, more to ensure the thieves' safety than out of fear of robberies. Trapmaster Tok is responsible for maintaining these traps, and such is the palace's reputation that thieves come to steal just for the reputation boost if they manage to escape alive. The Tally Wall is a memorial that began as graffiti with the names of thieves who perished in their robbery attempts, and a priest of Pharasma is hired to maintain the shrine. Payment for his services is a collaboration between the House and the Bloody Barbers, a cooperation that neither group emphasizes.
1944021219:
Tsukiwa

Tsukiwa (pronounced TSOO-key-wah) is a former mining town turned independent fortress located in western central Chu Ye, on the western end of the Nightford Mountains.
1945632189:
Maggot Tree

The Maggot Tree is in the center of the Hagwood, a huge, twisted tree that was once home to a coven of hags, of which only the Mother of Flies has survived. In 4710 AR, agents of the Council of Thieves attacked the tree, attempting to burn it down to destroy the hag who knew the only weakness in their new leader's plan.
1950701219:
Saresp Mountain

The cave-pocked Saresp Mountain, a peak in Mendev's far eastern reaches, was once the site of many ancient tombs as well as shelters for families seeking refuge from the harshest parts of winter. The caves hosted many Sarkorian refugees after the opening of the Worldwound, but all disappeared near the end of the Mendevian Crusades, their caves either sealed or apparently struck by grave robbers.
1957442699:
Parley Point

2 locations:

  • Pex
1961256189:
Green Market

The Green Market is a small, enclosed indoor market run by Zeeva Foxglove in Korvosa's South Shore neighborhood. It specializes in food, clothing, and other necessary items, but also carries jewelry and other luxuries for the upper classes. Its merchants are expressly forbidden to sell magic items.

1983752689:
Castle Tiapetra

The Castle Tiapetra is a wall fortress of the Chelaxian city of Corentyn, and features five central towers. All buildings of the castle have been converted to stables.

The Castle Tiapetra is one of the famous nine forts of Corentyn, according to the classical listing.
1987733069:
Alseen's Camels

Alseen's Camels sells Katapeshi camels at the Daystalls, breeds perfectly suited to this desert region. They are also very docile, making them easy for any rider. The owner, Alhamad Alseen, sells the camels along with all the necessary equipment to ride them properly.
2005506789:
Tekeh

Tekeh is a city in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.
2006177259:
Blisterwell

The settlement of Blisterwell was once an extensive dwarven quarry and strip mine, called Jerengoff, that fell under orc control in -3709 AR with the coming of the warlord Belkzen. It has stayed in orc hands ever since, but is now a hotly contested urban battleground between two orc holds: the Haskodars and the One Eyes.
2011345309:
Pashow

Pashow is the smallest of Thuvia's five allied city-states. It lies in the barren south of the country, in a location conveniently close to the Citadel of the Alchemist but lacking in natural resources. It was ruled by Emir Guldis until very recently. The city's inhabitants are known for their magical talents and revere Nethys as their patron deity. They use magic to help compensate for the harshness of the local environment.

2024317449:
Hoimpeia

The Iblydan city of Hoimpeia is the archipelago's primary port for westward commerce, and is a rival of Aelyosos, its counterpart to the west.
2048880169:
Nagura

Nagura is a small town in southern Minkai important as a trading post at the crossroads of three important highways.
2050798929:
Merab

Merab is the largest of Thuvia's five allied city-states, and is the closest thing the land has to a capital city. It is ruled by Ilepodus, the Patron of Merab, and has adopted Sarenrae as its patron deity, whose Temple of the Redeeming Sun is the largest temple to the Dawnflower—or to any deity—in the country.
2052647309:
Sleeper

The Sleeper is a gigantic monument located in the north-easternmost spur of the Mindspin Mountains within the Hold of Belkzen. It resembles an enormous dragon wrapped around a mountaintop. Steam or smoke seems to emanate from its massive maw, which is large enough to fit an entire house.

The sculpture contains a series of chambers coiled around the mountain peak that lead into its hollow interior. Beneath the Sleeper, deep in the heart of the mountain, is the ancient city of Xin-Gastash. The gigantic monument was the great entrance to the ancient capital of the Thassilonian realm of gluttony, Gastash.

Belkzen natives now call the sculpture the Sleeper. Only the bravest climb the mountain's slopes, much less enter the interior chambers. The local orcs, being naturally superstitious, stay away from the Sleeper and consider it a cursed ruin full of bad magic. The chambers within the Sleeper are mostly intact, although many of them show signs of earthquake damage.

2058079499:
Prismatic Lantern

The Prismatic Lantern is a monumental siege tower located on the Ustradi River near the city of Ecanus. It is equipped with magical weapons that, in times of war, target the Mana Wastes or Geb, unleashing multicolored beams of immensely powerful arcane energy. Designed by Archlord Alzika Karr, the tower became her tomb when she failed to predict that its energies could not be contained within the demiplane she had prepared. Even with her spectacular death, the clergy of Nethys continue to renew the wards each year, and the city's arcane college sends its graduates to witness its magnificence firsthand. The tower glows with magical energy constantly, and entry is only possible by flying, as those who cannot perform this basic form of magic do not deserve to experience the tower's wonders.
2058353969:
Walcourt

Walcourt was a lodge dedicated to Founder Crucisal that fell into disuse over the years. Located in Rego Laina, it was taken over by the Council of Thieves as one of their bases of operations. They possessed magical protections against divination, as well as allies infiltrated within other faiths who served as spies or fronts.
2058638849:
Grand Council Hall

The Grand Council Hall is a magnificent, large white marble structure where the city's most influential people meet to discuss the city's laws and administration. The 49 members of the Low Council meet monthly in a large hall with alcoves and stone thrones, where the acoustics provide good communication over long distances, though paradoxically, they also offer privacy for quiet conversations. Above these alcoves are the 13 seats of the High Council, which are in every respect superior to the others, and its members rarely appear in public except on the rare occasions when the entire body must vote on a motion, as they prefer to hold these debates in private rooms around the city.

The general public can attend if they possess one of the tablets granted to the councillors, which in theory should be given to the city's military and heroes, but in practice end up in the hands of corrupt merchants and nobles who want to be close to those in power to see if they can plunder something like vultures. A series of underground passages connect to factories and scriptoriums where the council's bureaucracy is on full display, and the entire system is protected by a private guard at the service of the institution itself.
2064321379:
Dabril

The town of Dabril is located on the Sellen River in the tumultuous nation of Galt, just south of Kallas Lake. Despite being on the river, the residents rarely work as river traders or fishermen. Instead, the people generally work as gardeners or perfumers, and the town has become known for its immense fields of roses and its manufacture of exotic perfumes.
2073433599:
Kovlar

Kovlar is a small dwarven city in Five Kings Mountains built in the tunnels and caverns at the southern outskirts of the fallen great city of Saggorak.
2080931129:
Skirgaard

Skirgaard was a frost giant village in the Mindspin Mountains whose leader, Skirkatla, raided the region centuries ago, both during her life and even after her death. The village sits at an elevation of 9,000 feet, resulting in permanently snow-covered terrain perfect for the giants who inhabit it.

At the base of the mountain, the temperature allows for cultivation, and there are several plantations of potatoes, onions, and wheat, as well as grasses that are harvested to feed the village's mammoths. Several giants from Volstus's armies trained in the village after Skirkatla allied herself with him during his conquest of Avistan.
2104793599:
Minaret

The Minaret is a domed tower of humanoid construction located high among the Fog Peaks of southern Galt. Rarely glimpsed only through powerful spyglasses, the origins and purpose of the Minaret remain unknown to the people of the nation.

No one is aware that the structure is of Qadiran origen and that it was a poorly worded wish that trapped a noble inside of the tower and displaced it all the way up here.
2110582819:
Weeping Fields

The Weeping Fields were once a plain with a verdant meadow, but after Earthfall, shadows filled the area and drained the vegetation of its color. It was here that the Black Triune met with Zon-Kuthon and signed the agreement that sealed the fate of all Nidal. Now, the Weeping Fields form the entry point to the Umbral Basin.

There are some vestiges of civilization on the plain, but shadowy predators and the soil that hinders agriculture make life difficult for the few who choose to live there. The Nidalese army, the Adamant Guard, makes more use of the plain, conducting military exercises and training recruits. At the end of their training, and after a grueling trial, successful recruits are able to join the Adamant Guard, where they earn the respect and fear of the rest of Nidal's inhabitants.
2111788569:
Aromas and Aphrodisiacs

Aromas and Aphrodisiacs is a pavilion of brightly colored tents connected to each other, with a scent that attracts anyone who approaches. The herbalist Chiaong runs the establishment with his slaves, offering herbs that can be inhaled, drunk, or applied to the skin to create a relaxing sensation in the user.
2126813509:
Emerald Chambers

The Emerald Chambers are a crypt and mausoleum dedicated to King Xin and the Runelords. It was built on the border between Cyrusian and Bakrakhan, now know as the Velashu Uplands as a place to commemorate the deeds of their master, though in reality it was these runelords who killed him, and the tomb served as a facade against Xin's devotees. An order of the king's servants and apprentices was created as the Silent Devotees, who cut out their tongues in a vow of silence for their lord. Among these devotees, Xin-Undoros was the most famous, having been very close to the king, and was devastated by his death, swearing to Lissala that he would protect the chambers for all eternity. Thousands of rooms connected by portals create a labyrinth that has killed more than one thief, though those who show true interest in or devotion to Xin are silently stalked by his defenders as long as they don't try to go further than the Hall of Xin's Memory. The walls and floor gleam as if made of emeralds, though it is nothing more than a worthless, artificial transmutation.
2128973559:
Gilded Shell

The Gilded Shell is a gigantic tortoise-shaped building situated in the Dawn Gate district of Katapesh. The surface of the building is covered with gleaming metal plates, designed and installed by a master armorer named Malthus Feyhew.

Feyhew and his staff of apprentices and journeymen craft high-quality and greatly valued suits of armor, all of masterwork quality. The master armorer tasks special interest in unusual materials, and keeps a standing offer to his clients to build custom armor from such materials at a 50-percent discount. He also keeps an inventory of special and unusual armors, including giant scorpion shells, blink dog hides, gorgon hides, gray render hides, Naga scales, bone mail, and wyvern hides.
2135100569:
Valley of the Pyramids

The Valley of the Pyramids, uncovered during a recent khamsin storm, is an unusual landscape of twisted, sand-carved canyons not fully seen since the time of the Four Pharaohs of Ascension. Rumors hold that the four pyramids contain magical portals that allow access to the extradimensional tombs of the Four Pharaohs. As of 4708 AR, artifacts from the tombs had already begun appearing in Sothis's bazaars.
2138910999:
Kozan

Kozan is a large port town on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, located about halfway between the capital of Oppara and Taldor's southern border with Qadira. Its nearest neighbors are Mut (to the north), and Merciful Bay (to the south).
